title: Kalandis IV

{{Quote|Last month, Tyionsis Cex makes planetfall and comes in after dropping off his latest batch of Imperial 'detainees' to the Prefect's tender, loving care.|Sub-Prefect Helkson Vall, extract from meeting with Intel Agent 438432|<ref name="WhatsInAName" />}}
'''Kalandis IV''' was an [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Pallis sector/Legends|Pallis sector]]. Once situated on the [[Itani Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]], during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the planet was the base of operations for [[Imperial Prefect|Prefect]] [[Ursellis]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. She cooperated with the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Tyionsis Cex]], who would receive Imperial [[Legal Authorization for Advanced Confinement Document]]s from [[Sub-Prefect]] [[Matagorn]] and deliver individuals captured on the basis of those documents back to Kalandis IV. When Matagorn once made a joke at Cex's expense during one such encounter, the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]] was offended, and several [[Standard day/Legends|days]] later, the sub-prefect disappeared and his body parts began surfacing across Kalandis IV.

==Description==
Kalandis IV was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="WhatsInAName" /> located in the [[Kalandis system/Legends|Kalandis system]],<ref name="TEA Kalandis" /> a part of the [[Pallis sector/Legends|Pallis sector]]<ref name="WhatsInAName" /> in the [[Trailing Sectors/Legends|Trailing Sectors]] portion of the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]].<ref name="TEA Kalandis" /> The planet occupied the fourth [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position in its [[Star system/Legends|system]].<ref name="WhatsInAName" />

Kalandis IV was once situated on the [[Itani Run]],<ref name="Itani" /> a [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] [[Star map/Legends|charted]] by the [[Scout/Legends|explorer]] [[Freia Kallea/Legends|Freia Kallea]] between [[3703 BBY]] and [[3702 BBY]]. The [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] route connected the Kalandis system to the [[Chardaan system/Legends|Chardaan]] and [[Itani system/Legends|Itani systems]]. Eventually, the set of hyperlanes scouted by Kallea was integrated into the [[Super-hyperroute/Legends|super-hyperroute]] known as the [[Hydian Way/Legends|Hydian Way]], which did not pass through the Kalandis system,<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> and the Itani Run became obsolete.<ref name="Platt">''[[Platt's Starport Guide]]''</ref>

==History==
===Cooperation on Kalandis IV===
{{Quote|We all know what Prefect Ursellis was like before Tremayne made LAACDocs a way of life. These handy-dandy incarceration tickets just gave her the ''carte blanche'' she'd been looking for to settle a few old scores and pack away a few folks indefinitely.|Sub-Prefect Helkson Vall, extract from meeting with Intel Agent 438432|<ref name="WhatsInAName" />}}
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], [[Imperial Prefect|Prefect]] [[Ursellis]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], along with her subordinates, [[Sub-Prefect]]s [[Matagorn]] and [[Helkson Vall]], were based on Kalandis IV. At some point, Ursellis became one<ref name="WhatsInAName" /> among Imperial officials who began issuing [[Legal Authorization for Advanced Confinement Document]]s—or LAACDocs for short—which were documents allowing the Empire to apprehend, detain, and confine individuals wanted for questioning.<ref name="GG10">''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]''</ref>

Subsequently, the [[Srrors'tok]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Tyionsis Cex]] was regularly employed by Ursellis for the capture of the individuals for whom the LAACDocs were issued. Cex would arrive on Kalandis IV with the captured individuals, deliver them to the Imperial authorities, and then receive a new batch of LAACDocs from Sub-Prefect Matagorn in person.<ref name="WhatsInAName" />

===Disappearance of Sub-Prefect Matagorn===
{{Quote|Anyway, Cex leaves, and the next day Matagorn suddenly gets word he's been okayed for that damn vacation he's been scratching about for months! Two days later, he disappears. The day after, a piece of him is located over in Gevis City, the next day one in Verdson, the next… well, you get the idea.|Sub-Prefect Helkson Vall, extract from meeting with Intel Agent 438432|<ref name="WhatsInAName" />}}
[[File:TyionsisCex-GG10.png|thumb|right|300px|On Kalandis IV, Sub-Prefect Matagorn had a fatal encounter with the bounty hunter Tyionsis Cex (pictured).]]
The adoption of the LAACDoc system led Matagorn to complain about the amount of work involved and to repeatedly request a vacation. One [[Standard day/Legends|day]], [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the introduction of the new system, the sub-prefect encountered Cex, who had come for the latest set of LAACDocs. Matagorn, exhausted from compiling the documents, handed the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]] the list and then made a joke by referring to Cex as the "personal LAACkey" of [[High Inquisitor]] [[Antinnis Tremayne]],<ref name="WhatsInAName" /> who had initiated the LAACDoc system.<ref name="GG10" /> In response, Cex, who was insulted by the joke's implication that he was someone's lackey, warned Matagorn that he had made a grave mistake and then left.<ref name="WhatsInAName" />

On the following day, Matagorn received notice about his vacation request finally being granted. Two days later, however, the sub-prefect disappeared, and over the subsequent days, his body parts began surfacing across Kalandis IV, including in [[Gevis City]] and [[Verdson]]. In the following month,<ref name="WhatsInAName" /> at some point between [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] and [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]'' is set between the events of {{Film|IV}} and {{Film|VI}}, which corresponds to the [[Time/Legends|time]] period between [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] and [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> Sub-Prefect Vall, who had witnessed Matagorn's last interaction with Cex, narrated the encounter during a confidential meeting with [[Intel Agent 438432]].<ref name="WhatsInAName" />

==Behind the scenes==
Kalandis IV was introduced in "[[What's In A Name]]," a short companion narrative included in the [[1994]] sourcebook ''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]''. The book was authored by [[Rick D. Stuart]] and published by [[West End Games]] for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="WhatsInAName" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Kalandis system, and therefore Kalandis IV, in grid square N-14.<ref name="TEA Kalandis" />
